sources.writer_profile: Alejandro Jiménez writes for Cordópolis, the Córdoba local section of elDiario.es. His food coverage focuses on neighbourhood dining near the city's historic patios, blending practical local knowledge with an appreciation for traditional cordobés cuisine. His writing reflects deep familiarity with the culinary geography of the old city.
sources.article_title: Dónde comer cerca de los Patios de Córdoba

sources.writer_profile: Txema Aguado is the creator and sole author of Yendo por la Vida, a Spanish food and travel blog specialising in fine dining experiences across Spain. His detailed multi-course restaurant reviews are known for their thoroughness, covering each dish, wine pairing, and service. His Choco review traces the restaurant's 50-year family history and its evolution toward Michelin recognition.
sources.article_title: Choco de Córdoba y su menú con aires cordobeses

sources.writer_profile: Molly Piccavey is the sole author of Piccavey.com, a food and travel site run from Andalusia, Spain, where she has lived since 1998. Her Córdoba restaurant guide draws on years of personal visits and local knowledge, covering everything from neighbourhood tapas bars to celebrated bodegas. Her writing emphasises independent personal experience rather than press trips.
sources.article_title: Cordoba Restaurants: Foodie Tips + Local Bites

sources.writer_profile: Hanne Olsen is the founder and author of Spain by Hanne, a Danish-language food and travel blog focused on the Iberian Peninsula. A frequent visitor to Córdoba, she writes for a Danish readership with first-hand restaurant recommendations based on multiple visits to the city. Her Córdoba guide emphasises the local tapas culture and historic taverns.
sources.article_title: Varieret andalusisk madscene: Her spiser du godt i Córdoba

sources.writer_profile: Brenda Alonso writes lifestyle and travel content for The Objective, a Spanish digital news and opinion publication. Her 2023 Córdoba restaurant guide covers establishments across the city's historic centre, focusing on traditional cordobés cuisine and the neighbourhood dining scene. Her coverage highlights signature dishes and the authentic atmosphere of each taberna.
sources.article_title: Dónde comer en Córdoba: 13 restaurantes para disfrutar de los manjares de la ciudad

sources.publication: El Viaje Me Hizo a Mí
sources.writer_profile: Jose López writes the Spanish travel blog El Viaje Me Hizo a Mí, which covers budget-friendly travel and local dining across Spain. His Córdoba restaurant guide, based on personal experience living in the city, covers fifteen tapas bars and restaurants in the historic centre, with an emphasis on authentic neighbourhood spots that locals frequent.
sources.article_title: 15 sitios para comer y tapear por el centro de Córdoba

sources.writer_profile: Morgane Mazelier is a French travel writer and founder of Andalucía Lovers, a detailed regional food and travel guide covering southern Spain. Her Córdoba restaurant reviews are based on first-hand visits and reflect a food-tourism perspective, with particular attention to the historic dining scene and contemporary creative restaurants. The guide was updated in January 2026.
sources.article_title: 15 best restaurants in Cordoba (2026): our reviews

sources.writer_profile: M. Ángeles Ramírez covers the Córdoba food and lifestyle scene for El Debate's local La Voz de Córdoba section. Her restaurant coverage tracks the city's gastronomic trends, from traditional tabernas to emerging fine dining establishments seeking Michelin recognition. She writes with knowledge of the local restaurant industry and its business dynamics.
sources.article_title: La gastronomía cordobesa, más de moda que nunca: 5 restaurantes donde comprobarlo

restaurants.name: Bar Santos
restaurants.quote: This classic pick next to the Mosque is a tiny bar serving fluffy tortilla española. With standing room only this has become a must do when visiting Cordoba. Locals and visitors pop over to have a cold beer and slice of tortilla as an appetiser.
restaurants.article_title: Cordoba Restaurants: Foodie Tips + Local Bites

restaurants.name: Casa Pepe de La Judería
restaurants.quote: Ubicada en el corazón del histórico barrio judío, Casa Pepe de La Judería te invita a un viaje culinario a través de la tradición cordobesa. Su plato estrella es el rabo de toro, un guiso tierno y sabroso que refleja la esencia de la cocina local.
restaurants.article_title: Dónde comer en Córdoba: 13 restaurantes para disfrutar de los manjares de la ciudad

restaurants.name: Almatea
restaurants.quote: This quirky vegetarian restaurant along the river bank was a welcoming and healthy option. Despite it being vegetable based food it was flavourful, filling and interesting to select from the menu. Can't recommend this enough.
restaurants.article_title: Cordoba Restaurants: Foodie Tips + Local Bites

restaurants.name: La Regadera
restaurants.quote: This modern restaurant is not far from the Plaza del Potro. We had a bowl of thick creamy Salmorejo with finely cut Jamon Serrano on top. The main course was crispy Fried Cod fish — the batter was perfectly done and golden.
restaurants.article_title: Cordoba Restaurants: Foodie Tips + Local Bites

restaurants.name: Taberna La Viuda
restaurants.quote: Blandingen af den gode mad, der leder tankerne mod bedstemors kødgryder og den usædvanlig nærværende betjening gør, at jeg gerne vender tilbage.
restaurants.article_title: Varieret andalusisk madscene: Her spiser du godt i Córdoba

restaurants.name: El Astronauta
restaurants.quote: This cool restaurant offers a midweek menu for 10 euros. Located just below the Roma Temple they have plenty of healthy options. There are also quite a few vegetarian dishes to choose from.
restaurants.article_title: Cordoba Restaurants: Foodie Tips + Local Bites

restaurants.name: Taberna Montillana
restaurants.quote: We had to try a Cordoba Flamenquín so after getting a couple of ice cold beers we ordered. Some crispy regañas were placed on our table by the friendly waiter. The flamenquin was delicious — nothing fancy, just traditional Cordoba bar food. The wine selection is huge.
restaurants.article_title: Cordoba Restaurants: Foodie Tips + Local Bites

restaurants.name: Taberna Los Merengueles
dishes_mentioned: huevos rotos con jamón y patatas
restaurants.quote: El plato de huevos estrellados elaborado con huevos rotos con jamón y patatas es una opción indulgente que seguramente satisfará tus antojos. El servicio amable y rápido es otro aspecto destacado de este lugar.
restaurants.article_title: Dónde comer en Córdoba: 13 restaurantes para disfrutar de los manjares de la ciudad

restaurants.name: Sociedad Plateros María Auxiliadora
restaurants.quote: Uno de los lugares más históricos, emblemáticos y relevantes de la gastronomía cordobesa, con platos como el salmorejo, las berenjenas fritas con miel o el flamenquín.
restaurants.article_title: Dónde comer cerca de los Patios de Córdoba

restaurants.name: La Contentura
restaurants.quote: Su punto fuerte es el picoteo, con platos clásicos como el salmorejo, la carne en salsa al vino o las croquetas de rabo de toro.
restaurants.article_title: Dónde comer cerca de los Patios de Córdoba

restaurants.name: Taberna La Sacristía
restaurants.quote: Sus montaditos de pringá, elaborados con recetas originales y horas de cocción, acompañan a guisos tradicionales como los callos o la carrillada.
restaurants.article_title: Dónde comer cerca de los Patios de Córdoba

restaurants.name: El Patio de María
restaurants.quote: Destaca por platos como su mazamorra caramelizada, los flamenquines, el rabo de toro y las alcachofas confitadas con foie, con precios de 8 a 18 euros.
restaurants.article_title: Dónde comer cerca de los Patios de Córdoba

restaurants.name: Mesón San Basilio
restaurants.quote: Si buscas comida casera típica de Córdoba, aquí encontrarás rabo de toro, salmorejo, pisto cordobés y flamenquín, junto a pescados, carnes y revueltos.
restaurants.article_title: Dónde comer cerca de los Patios de Córdoba

restaurants.name: La Tinaja
dishes_mentioned: cod filled ravioli in coconut sauce
restaurants.quote: Service at this Riverside Restaurant was faultless. For starters I chose the obligatory Aubergines with Honey which is so typical in Cordoba. My main course was a Cod filled ravioli in coconut sauce. Cost around 28 euros per person for three courses.
restaurants.article_title: Cordoba Restaurants: Foodie Tips + Local Bites

restaurants.name: Taberna del Rio
restaurants.quote: Offering delicious typical delicacies such as salmorejo and local cheeses from Cordoba province, its highly recommended. They also have local wines such as Montilla Moriles which is worth trying.
restaurants.article_title: Cordoba Restaurants: Foodie Tips + Local Bites

restaurants.name: La Boca
restaurants.quote: They offer a vegetarian daily menu but also have meat options. On the day I dined there it was three courses: a chilli bean stew followed by a rocket thin crust pizza. Good value for money.
restaurants.article_title: Cordoba Restaurants: Foodie Tips + Local Bites

restaurants.name: Bodegas Campos
restaurants.quote: The deep ruby red colour of the gazpacho was like no other. Living in Andalusia I've tasted a lot of gazpacho but this one was something special. The service was professional and polished — it's one of the Cordoba restaurants I'd like to return to sometime.
restaurants.article_title: Cordoba Restaurants: Foodie Tips + Local Bites

restaurants.name: La Maceta
restaurants.quote: Den lille tapasbar er lyst og moderne indrettet med højborde og franske metal-barstole ved vinduet.
restaurants.article_title: Varieret andalusisk madscene: Her spiser du godt i Córdoba

restaurants.name: La Cazuela de la Esparteria
restaurants.quote: Menuen er fyldt med lokale, typiske tapas og retter som små fritterede fisk, salmorejo, fyldte muslinger, flamenquín cordobés.
restaurants.article_title: Varieret andalusisk madscene: Her spiser du godt i Córdoba
restaurants.geo_matched_name: Taberna La Cazuela de la Esparteria

restaurants.name: Taberna El Nº10
restaurants.quote: No puedes dejar de probar su salmorejo cordobés, una crema fría de tomate y pan, acompañada de jamón serrano y huevo duro. Su amplia selección de vinos locales complementa a la perfección cada bocado.
restaurants.article_title: Dónde comer en Córdoba: 13 restaurantes para disfrutar de los manjares de la ciudad

restaurants.name: Taberna El Abanico
restaurants.quote: La especialidad de la casa son las berenjenas con miel de caña, una deliciosa combinación de sabores dulces y salados que no debes perderte.
restaurants.article_title: Dónde comer en Córdoba: 13 restaurantes para disfrutar de los manjares de la ciudad

restaurants.name: Casa Rubio
restaurants.quote: Sus flamenquines caseros, rollitos rellenos de jamón y queso, son una elección popular entre los comensales. El ambiente clásico andaluz del restaurante crea un ambiente acogedor para disfrutar de una comida memorable.
restaurants.article_title: Dónde comer en Córdoba: 13 restaurantes para disfrutar de los manjares de la ciudad

restaurants.name: Los Patios de La Marquesa
dishes_mentioned: flor de berenjena rellena de rabo de toro
restaurants.signature_dish: flor de berenjena rellena de rabo de toro
restaurants.quote: Inmerso en un entorno histórico, Los Patios de La Marquesa ofrece una experiencia gastronómica elegante. Su plato estrella, la flor de berenjena rellena de rabo de toro, combina sabores tradicionales con una presentación sofisticada.
restaurants.article_title: Dónde comer en Córdoba: 13 restaurantes para disfrutar de los manjares de la ciudad

restaurants.name: Taberna Góngora
restaurants.quote: Ubicada cerca de la Mezquita-Catedral, Taberna Góngora es una joya gastronómica que captura el espíritu de la ciudad. Sus flamenquines de Córdoba son un placer crujiente y sabroso que no debes perderte.
restaurants.article_title: Dónde comer en Córdoba: 13 restaurantes para disfrutar de los manjares de la ciudad

restaurants.name: Bodegas Mezquita Corregidor
restaurants.quote: Su plato insignia, el salmorejo con jamón y huevo, te permitirá degustar una de las delicias más icónicas de Córdoba. El ambiente rústico de la bodega agrega un toque especial a tu visita.
restaurants.article_title: Dónde comer en Córdoba: 13 restaurantes para disfrutar de los manjares de la ciudad

restaurants.name: Garum 2.1
restaurants.quote: Si buscas una perspectiva moderna de la gastronomía cordobesa, Garum 2.1 es el lugar indicado. Su plato distintivo, el salmorejo de mango con langostinos, es una reinterpretación refrescante de un clásico.
restaurants.article_title: Dónde comer en Córdoba: 13 restaurantes para disfrutar de los manjares de la ciudad

restaurants.name: Taberna Salinas
dishes_mentioned: naranjas picás con aceite y bacalao
restaurants.quote: Está muy cerca de la plaza de la Corredera y su carta es más variada que la media, con platos como la carne con tomate, las setas a la plancha y las naranjas picás con aceite y bacalao.
restaurants.article_title: 15 sitios para comer y tapear por el centro de Córdoba

restaurants.name: La Taberna de Almodovar
restaurants.quote: Nestled in an alleyway in the historic center, La Taberna de Almodovar embodies the very essence of the traditional Cordovan tavern. On our visit, we began our meal with the inevitable croquetas with serrano ham and a lomo de atun.
restaurants.article_title: 15 best restaurants in Cordoba (2026): our reviews

restaurants.name: Arbequina
dishes_mentioned: beef sirloin steak with truffled celery root cream
restaurants.quote: Located in the elegant setting of the Hotel Hospes Palacio del Bailío, Restaurant Arbequina offers refined cuisine. During our visit, we were delighted by the beef sirloin steak with truffled celery root cream.
restaurants.article_title: 15 best restaurants in Cordoba (2026): our reviews

restaurants.name: Casa de Manolete Bistró
dishes_mentioned: beet salmorejo with tuna ventrèche confit and capers
restaurants.signature_dish: beet salmorejo with tuna ventrèche confit and capers
restaurants.quote: Housed in an old 16th-century mansion, Casa de Manolete Bistró pays tribute to the famous Cordoban bullfighter. The chef offers a creative version of beet salmorejo, served with tuna ventrèche confit and capers.
restaurants.article_title: 15 best restaurants in Cordoba (2026): our reviews

restaurants.name: Almudaina
restaurants.quote: Located in a historic building in the Judéría district, this restaurant serves refined Andalucia cuisine. The menu features local specialties such as salmorejo, a long-simmered bull's tail, and flamenquín.
restaurants.article_title: 15 best restaurants in Cordoba (2026): our reviews

restaurants.name: Asador Central
restaurants.quote: In the heart of Cordoba, Asador Central has earned a reputation for excellence thanks to its mastery of wood-fired cooking. We were impressed by the quality of the meats, especially the T-bone beef.
restaurants.article_title: 15 best restaurants in Cordoba (2026): our reviews

restaurants.name: El Lindero Gastro Taberna
restaurants.quote: El Lindero Gastro Taberna's modern, uncluttered setting is a showcase for local Cordoban produce. The restaurant is renowned for its selection of Iberian meats, including 100% bellota solomillo ibérico.
restaurants.article_title: 15 best restaurants in Cordoba (2026): our reviews

restaurants.name: Atmósfera Gastrobar
dishes_mentioned: salmorejo with olive oil ice cream
restaurants.signature_dish: salmorejo with olive oil ice cream
restaurants.quote: Open since November 2023, Atmosphera Gastrobar breathes new life into Cordoba's gastronomic scene. The kitchen is overseen by chef Miguel Ángel Bejarano, who has worked with such greats as Dabiz Muñoz. We were seduced by their interpretation of salmorejo served with olive oil ice cream.
restaurants.article_title: 15 best restaurants in Cordoba (2026): our reviews

restaurants.name: La Esquinita de la Judería
name_variants: La Esquinita de la Juderia el patio
restaurants.quote: Located just a stone's throw from the Mosque-Cathedral, in the historic Judería district, this restaurant occupies a remarkable building constructed on top of still-visible Roman remains. The menu features the Flamenquín Mozárabe, garnished with caramelized peppers, goat's cheese, Iberian ham and dried fruit.
restaurants.article_title: 15 best restaurants in Cordoba (2026): our reviews

restaurants.name: Tu Pescaito
restaurants.quote: Located on the Plaza de la Corredera, one of Cordoba's most emblematic landmarks, Tu Pescaito offers a fusion of Cordovan and Portuguese cuisine, with codfish on the menu. The restaurant's main offering is seafood dishes sourced directly from the Sánchez Peña Market.
restaurants.article_title: 15 best restaurants in Cordoba (2026): our reviews

restaurants.name: La Compañía
restaurants.quote: This traditional Cordovan tavern, nestled in the Judería district, won us over with its authenticity. The menu focuses on typical Spanish tapas such as croquetas, patatas bravas, bull meat and the famous cold Cordovan soup: salmorejo.
restaurants.article_title: 15 best restaurants in Cordoba (2026): our reviews

restaurants.name: La Lianta de Quini
dishes_mentioned: almond mazamorra with green apple and smoked sardines
restaurants.signature_dish: almond mazamorra with green apple and smoked sardines
restaurants.quote: This contemporary and relaxed restaurant offers a creative take on Andalucia cuisine. The menu features original creations such as almond mazamorra served with green apple and smoked sardines.
restaurants.article_title: 15 best restaurants in Cordoba (2026): our reviews

restaurants.name: Terra Olea
restaurants.quote: El establecimiento capitaneado por el chef Paco Villar, uno de los favoritos de la ciudad para alzarse con una Estrella Michelín en los próximos años.
restaurants.article_title: La gastronomía cordobesa, más de moda que nunca: 5 restaurantes donde comprobarlo

restaurants.name: Lamundi
restaurants.quote: En pleno centro de la ciudad se localiza este local, donde destaca el risotto de rabo de toro como uno de sus platos más recomendados.
restaurants.article_title: La gastronomía cordobesa, más de moda que nunca: 5 restaurantes donde comprobarlo
